sections.paragraph:
ἄδοξος, ον without reputation, obscure (so TGF p. 921, adespota no. 423; X.+; OGI 5, 63f [311 b.c.]; POxy 79 II, 4 [II a.d.]; Sir 10:31; Jos., Vi. 278; Just.) 1 Cl 3:3; MPol 8:1 (both times as opp. of ἔνδοξος, as Just., D. 32, 1; Ael. Aristid. 26, 39 K.=14 p. 338 D.; Diog. L. 7, 117).—DELG s.v. δοκάω.

TGF TGF = Tragicorum Graecorum Fragmenta—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 21
